message:
feat(conformance): add resource_ops executor (bd-mwdy)

Add execute_getrlimit_case handler for resource_ops family.
Calls frankenlibc_abi::resource_abi::getrlimit and returns result.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-mjon","title":"bd-yos subtask: Thread bootstrap trampoline finalization for pthread_create","description":"Background:
- Thread bootstrap is a core blocker for both callthrough removal and TLS key APIs.

Goal:
- Finalize clone/trampoline startup path for pthread_create with explicit invariants and ABI boundaries.

Deliverables:
1) Thread start trampoline contract (entry args, return handling, cleanup path).
2) Parent/child synchronization at startup.
3) Error path behavior and errno propagation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- pthread_create success/failure semantics match declared support matrix behavior.
- No hidden host-libc dependency in startup path for covered mode/profile.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for bootstrap components and error branches.
- Structured logs: trace_id, thread_id, parent_id, start_state, exit_state, errno, timing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonForge","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.822186049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139407950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139340033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":1123,"issue_id":"bd-mjon","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Thread bootstrap trampoline implementation.

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/raw.rs: Added clone_thread_asm (inline asm trampoline for Linux clone syscall)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added SYS_GETTID, SYS_SET_TID_ADDRESS, sys_gettid, sys_exit_thread, sys_clone_thread
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: Complete native thread creation via clone (ThreadHandle, create_thread, join_thread, detach_thread, self_tid)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mod.rs: Updated re-exports

Quality gates:
- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-core: PASS
- cargo check -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets: PASS
- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings: PASS
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::thread: 6/6 PASS

Tests:
- create_and_join_thread_returns_value
- child_thread_can_write_shared_memory
- multiple_threads_created_and_joined
- detach_thread_does_not_error
- join_null_handle_returns_einval
- gettid_returns_positive

Architecture:
- Uses CLONE_VM|CLONE_FS|CLONE_FILES|CLONE_SIGHAND|CLONE_THREAD|CLONE_SYSVSEM|CLONE_SETTLS|CLONE_PARENT_SETTID|CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID
- Child stack: [fn_ptr][arg] layout, trampoline pops both, calls fn_ptr(arg), SYS_exit on return
- Join uses futex-wait on TID (kernel clears via CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID and wakes waiters)
- Parent/child sync via startup futex (ensures args consumed before create returns)
- 2MiB stack with 4KiB guard page via mmap","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mtj","title":"EPIC: E2E Reality Gate (build + LD_PRELOAD smoke + ABI surface)","description":"Critique mapping: #1.

Goal: make glibc_rust indisputably real by proving it builds and runs on real binaries.

Deliverables:
- CI/local gate: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + cdylib build.
- ABI surface reports: exported symbol list + versioning + support matrix.
- LD_PRELOAD smoke harness for strict+hardened.

Acceptance:
- One command runs the full gate and returns non-zero on any drift/regression.
- Failures produce a deterministic crash bundle (backtrace + evidence snapshot).

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:06.684806153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:07.346791885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:07.346754184Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critique","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2cr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2p0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-3ex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-3jh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-6yd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-nklt","title":"D.2: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement gai_suspend() which waits for one or more async DNS requests to complete, with optional timeout.

## POSIX Signature
```c
int gai_suspend(const struct gaicb *const list[], int nitems, const struct timespec *timeout);
```

Returns:
- 0: at least one request completed
- EAI_AGAIN: timeout expired before any completion
- EAI_ALLDONE: all requests already completed
- EAI_SYSTEM: error (errno set)

## Design
1. Scan the request list — if any are already completed (gai_error != EAI_INPROGRESS), return 0 immediately
2. If all are completed, return EAI_ALLDONE
3. Otherwise, wait using a futex on a shared completion counter:
   - Each request increments a completion futex when it transitions out of IN_PROGRESS
   - gai_suspend does FUTEX_WAIT on the counter with timeout
   - On wake, re-scan the list for completed requests
4. If timeout is NULL, wait indefinitely (block until at least one completes)
5. If timeout expires, return EAI_AGAIN

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs
- Replace gai_suspend body
- Add ASYNC_DNS_COMPLETION_FUTEX: AtomicU32 (incremented on each completion)
- Use SYS_futex for timed wait

## Testing
- All requests already done: returns EAI_ALLDONE
- Empty list (nitems=0): returns EAI_ALLDONE
- Null list: returns EAI_SYSTEM with EINVAL
- Timeout with no completions: returns EAI_AGAIN (use short 1ms timeout)
- One request completes immediately: returns 0

## Acceptance Criteria
gai_suspend correctly waits and returns appropriate status codes. Timeout is respected. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:58.266246587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:22.166397683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.578636240Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No gai_suspend futex wait needed — function returns immediately since no async requests exist.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}
 {"id":"bd-oai","title":"Epic: Dual-Mode Semantics & Deterministic Replay","description":"Dual-mode execution: Strict (default, ABI-compatible, no repair, <20ns) and Hardened (deterministic repair per policy tables, structured JSON evidence per repair, <200ns). Mode selected immutably at process startup for deterministic replay. RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154-field state capture). Evidence symbol records with RaptorQ-inspired redundancy.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Session 30: Epic closed — all children completed

All child beads closed:
- bd-oai.1: Dual-mode selection + strict/hardened execution paths ✅
- bd-oai.2: RuntimeKernelSnapshot (165+ fields) + deterministic replay ✅  
- bd-oai.3: Evidence symbol records + RaptorQ-inspired redundancy ✅

Verification:
- 16 E2E tests in runtime_math_dual_mode_e2e_test.rs (all pass)
- 33 evidence system tests (all pass)
- Deterministic replay produces identical decision streams and JSONL logs
- Branch-diversity enforcement: Healthy/NearViolation/Violation states verified
- Golden snapshot regression gate: JSON equality under deterministic inputs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:48.791246202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:40.030481360Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:32Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","dual-mode","epic","frankenlibc","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1124,"issue_id":"bd-oai","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"}]}

These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.

 {"id":"bd-ta3b","title":"C.1: Implement gshadow line parser in frankenlibc-core","description":"## Goal
Add a gshadow line parser to frankenlibc-core, following the exact pattern of the existing /etc/passwd parser in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

## Reference: Existing Pattern
The passwd parser at pwd/mod.rs uses:
- `pub struct Passwd { pw_name: Vec<u8>, pw_passwd: Vec<u8>, ... }`
- `pub fn parse_passwd_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — splits on colons, validates field count
- `pub fn lookup_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — iterates lines, matches name

The gshadow parser should follow this EXACT pattern for consistency.

## gshadow File Format (/etc/gshadow)
Each line: `group_name:encrypted_password:administrators:members`
- group_name: non-empty, no colons (same as /etc/group name field)
- encrypted_password: may be empty (""), or "!" (locked), or "*" (no password)
- administrators: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no admins)
- members: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no members)

Example lines:
  root:*::
  shadow:!::
  sudo:!::alice,bob
  dev:x:alice:alice,bob,charlie

## Implementation

### File: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs (NEW FILE)
Add `pub mod gshadow;` to crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

### Struct
```rust
pub struct Gshadow {
    pub sg_namp: Vec<u8>,    // group name
    pub sg_passwd: Vec<u8>,  // encrypted password
    pub sg_adm: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated admin list
    pub sg_mem: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated member list
}
```

### Functions
```rust
pub fn parse_gshadow_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
pub fn lookup_gshadow_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
```

### Parsing Logic
1. Skip empty lines and lines starting with #
2. Split on b':' — must have exactly 4 fields
3. Field 0 (name) must be non-empty
4. Fields 1-3 may be empty
5. Return Gshadow with owned Vec<u8> copies

## Inline Tests (#[cfg(test)])
- parse_valid_full_line: `sudo:!:admin1,admin2:user1,user2` -> all 4 fields correct
- parse_minimal_line: `root:*::` -> password="*", admins="", members=""
- parse_empty_password: `grp:::` -> password is empty
- parse_locked_password: `grp:!::` -> password is "!"
- parse_with_admins_and_members: `dev:x:alice:alice,bob` -> admins="alice", members="alice,bob"
- reject_too_few_fields: `root:*:` (3 colons needed, only 2) -> None
- reject_empty_name: `:*::` -> None
- reject_comment_line: `# comment` -> None
- reject_empty_line: "" -> None
- lookup_finds_entry: content with 3 lines, lookup "sudo" finds correct one
- lookup_returns_none_for_missing: lookup "nonexistent" returns None
- lookup_is_case_sensitive: "ROOT" does not match "root"

## Acceptance Criteria
Parser handles all gshadow field combinations. 12 inline tests pass. cargo clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:37.066192744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.720091402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.719809839Z","close_reason":"Implemented gshadow parser in frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs. Struct Gshadow with sg_namp/sg_passwd/sg_adm/sg_mem fields. parse_gshadow_line() handles comments, blank lines, field validation. lookup_gshadow_by_name() scans content for case-sensitive name match. 14 inline tests pass covering: valid full/minimal lines, empty/locked password, admin+member lists, reject malformed, lookup hit/miss/case sensitivity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pwd","stub-elimination"]}

 {"id":"bd-thm2","title":"Fix c11threads managed-attr/host-pthread init mismatch","description":"c11threads_abi::mtx_init was building a FrankenLibC-managed pthread mutexattr word and passing it into host pthread_mutex_init, which triggered a glibc __pthread_tpp_change_priority assertion in c11threads_abi_test. Fix the C11 mutex/condvar init lane so C11 synchronization objects are initialized consistently on the native-managed path, then verify the focused C11 suite and workspace ABI checks via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFrog","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:34:41.394889788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204943932Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204703667Z","close_reason":"Resolved C11 mutex/condvar init mismatch by keeping C11 synchronization initialization on the native-managed lane; focused c11threads tests now pass and workspace tests advance to unrelated dlfcn failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1358,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented scoped native-mutex delegation helpers in pthread_abi and switched c11threads mutex/condvar init to use the native-managed lane. Verification: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test test_mtx_init_lock_unlock_destroy -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed; `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed (36/36); `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed; `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` completed without diffs. Workspace-wide `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` is still running under rch session 98302.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:36:15Z"},{"id":1359,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Broader verification update: `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets` no longer aborts in c11threads; the next remaining workspace failure is unrelated in `-p frankenlibc-abi --test dlfcn_abi_test` (14 failures, first at `dlerror_consumed_after_read`, then lock poisoning across dlopen/dlsym/dlvsym tests). This confirms the c11threads glibc assertion is resolved.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54Z"}]}
